source: Compiled from VA.gov, DOD, Syracuse IVMF, and veteran career resources

postNine11.eligibility: 90+ days active duty after 9/11/2001 (100% at 36 months)
benefits: Full tuition at public institutions (or Yellow Ribbon for private)
postNine11.yellowRibbon: Voluntary program where schools match VA funding for tuition above the public school cap. Most T50 schools participate.
postNine11.transferability: Can transfer to spouse or dependents if 6+ years served with 4+ more committed
postNine11.expirationExtension: No expiration for service members discharged on/after Jan 1, 2013 (Forever GI Bill)

vocRehab.name: VR&E (Veteran Readiness and Employment, Chapter 31)
vocRehab.benefits: More generous than GI Bill: no time limit on education, covers all costs + monthly subsistence
vocRehab.bestFor: Veterans with service-connected disabilities wanting career change

strategy: Use VR&E first if eligible (saves GI Bill for later or to transfer to dependents)
Community college + transfer can maximize GI Bill months
Some states offer additional veteran education benefits (TX, CA, IL, NY are most generous)

programs: PMP certification (military experience counts toward hours)
topInDemandPaths.note: Military leadership experience translates directly. PMP is the gold standard.

mosTranslation: 68C Nurse → Civilian RN (some states allow direct licensure)
All → PA School (patient care hours count)
programs: PA school (military medics have strong apps)
Nursing BSN programs with military credits

mosTranslation: Any → GS positions with veteran preference
MPs/CID → Federal law enforcement (FBI, DEA, Secret Service)
programs: Direct hire authority for certain agencies
topInDemandPaths.note: Veterans get 5-point or 10-point preference (disabled) on federal hiring. Significant advantage.

funding: Hivers and Strivers (angel network for military grads)
topInDemandPaths.avgSalary: Varies widely, $40K-$200K+ depending on business success

transitionResources.skillBridge: Last 180 days of service: work with civilian employer while still receiving military pay/benefits. 2,800+ employers participate.
transitionResources.taps: Transition Assistance Program: mandatory pre-separation workshops. Start 12-18 months before ETS.
resumeTips: Translate military jargon to civilian language (use O*NET crosswalk)
Quantify: '35 personnel' → 'team of 35 direct reports', '$2M budget' → '$2M annual operating budget'
Lead with transferable skills: leadership, logistics, project management, team building
Security clearance IS a resume bullet — mention it prominently if active

securityClearance.value: Active TS/SCI clearance adds $10K-$30K to salary. Some employers will hire solely for clearance.
securityClearance.timeline: Secret: 3-6 months. Top Secret: 6-18 months. TS/SCI: 12-24 months.
securityClearance.retention: Clearance stays active 2 years after separation (24 months). After that, reinvestigation required.
